message:
Last week my partner made fun of me for having a whole drawer dedicated to flashlights and battery packs. Then our power went out for six hours, and suddenly I was the hero with a fully charged lantern, fresh AAs, and enough USB power to keep both our phones alive.

I'm not saying I *planned* to look smug about it. But I definitely did.

The thing is, I'm not some paranoid doomsday person—I just got tired of scrambling in the dark during past outages, hunting for dead batteries and praying my phone didn't die before the power came back. So I started keeping a little "light and charge" stash. Nothing fancy. Just one or two good flashlights, a hand-crank lantern for the kitchen table, and a couple of power banks that I actually remember to top up.

Turns out, being the gloriously over-prepared person in the room feels pretty great.

Do you have a go-to spot for flashlights and power in your home, or are you still in the "find-it-in-a-blackout" era like I used to be?
